red_flags.name: Acute Chest Pain with Vital Signs Changes
Altered Consciousness or Confusion
red_flags.escalation_reason: Life-threatening condition - possible stroke, intracranial hemorrhage, severe hypoglycemia

red_flags.name: Severe Bleeding or Hemorrhage
red_flags.action: Emergency services, direct pressure to wound

red_flags.name: Sudden Severe Headache
red_flags.escalation_reason: Possible meningitis, subarachnoid hemorrhage, hypertensive emergency

red_flags.name: Severe Shortness of Breath
red_flags.escalation_reason: Respiratory failure risk - PE, pneumonia, asthma exacerbation

red_flags.name: Hypertensive Crisis
red_flags.escalation_reason: Hypertensive emergency with end-organ damage risk

red_flags.name: Severe Hypoglycemia with Altered Mental Status
red_flags.escalation_reason: Risk of brain damage, seizure, death
red_flags.action: Immediate glucose therapy, hospital admission if unresponsive

red_flags.name: Typical Anginal Pain by History
red_flags.escalation_reason: Typical anginal character — must escalate even if ECG/Echo/troponin are normal (unstable angina presents this way)
red_flags.action: Hospital referral for angiography per ACC/AHA 2023; do not let normal initial tests reassure

red_flags.name: Atypical ACS in High-Risk Patient
red_flags.escalation_reason: Atypical ACS presentation in high-risk patient (women, elderly, diabetics often present without classic chest pain)
red_flags.action: Treat as ACS until proven otherwise; arrange transport

red_flags.name: Major Trauma
red_flags.escalation_reason: Major trauma — primary survey and rapid transport per ATLS
red_flags.action: ABC primary survey, control bleeding, immobilize C-spine, arrange transport, do NOT remove impaled objects

red_flags.name: Acute Poisoning / Ingestion
red_flags.escalation_reason: Acute poisoning — antidote may be time-critical; do not induce vomiting
red_flags.action: Hospital transport, bring the container/label, recovery position if unconscious, maintain airway

red_flags.name: Organophosphate Pesticide Toxicity
red_flags.escalation_reason: Cholinergic crisis — atropine is the antidote, time-critical
red_flags.action: Hospital transport, atropine if clinician trained, decontaminate skin if topical exposure

red_flags.name: Snake Bite
red_flags.escalation_reason: Potential envenomation — neurotoxic effects can have 30–60 min lag; transport for anti-venom
red_flags.action: Immobilize limb below heart level, remove constricting items, transport now. DO NOT apply tourniquet, DO NOT cut/suck the wound, DO NOT wait for symptoms.
